content: I just finished reading 'The Power of Now' by Eckhart Tolle. It's really making me think about my life and priorities.
That's a great book! What specifically resonated with you from the book?
I think it's the idea of living in the present moment. As an Emergency Room Nurse at City Hospital, I see a lot of suffering and it's easy to get caught up in worries about the future or past.
That makes sense. It can be challenging to stay present in a high-stress environment like an ER. Do you find that your work affects your personal life or hobbies, like salsa dancing?
Actually, salsa dancing is a great way for me to unwind and stay present. I've been taking classes at a local studio on Wednesday nights for the past 6 months.
That sounds like a great way to relax! Have you considered incorporating some of the principles from 'The Power of Now' into your daily life, like meditation or mindfulness practices?
I've been trying to meditate for 10 minutes each morning, but it's hard to stick to it. I've also been thinking about starting a YouTube channel to share my thoughts on philosophy and life.
Starting a YouTube channel can be a great way to share your ideas and connect with others. What kind of content do you think you'll be creating for your channel?

I'm thinking of doing a mix of video essays and interviews with other philosophers and thought leaders. I've already spoken with a few potential guests, including my friend Dr. Rachel Kim, who's a philosophy professor at NYU.
That sounds like a fascinating project! Have you considered reaching out to any other experts in the field, like authors or researchers?

Yes, I've been reading a lot of books on philosophy and psychology, including 'Man's Search for Meaning' by Viktor Frankl. I'd love to have him on my channel, but unfortunately, he passed away in 1997.
That's a great book! Viktor Frankl's work is really inspiring. Maybe you could consider having a guest who's been influenced by his work or is continuing his legacy in some way?

That's a great idea! I'll have to look into it. By the way, I'm 57 years old and feeling like I'm just getting started on this new project. It's exciting to think about what the next few years will bring!
It's never too late to start something new, and it's great that you're feeling energized and motivated. What are your plans for the next few months in terms of getting your channel up and running?

I'm hoping to launch my channel by the end of March, so I've got about 6 weeks to get everything ready. I'm planning to release a new video every 2 weeks, and I've already outlined my first 6 episodes.
That sounds like a solid plan! Do you have any idea how you'll be promoting your channel or getting the word out to potential viewers?

I've been thinking about using social media, like Twitter and Instagram, to promote my channel. I've also been considering reaching out to some of my colleagues at City Hospital to see if they'd be interested in sharing my content with their networks.
Using social media and leveraging your existing network can be a great way to get started. Good luck with your channel, and I hope it brings you a lot of joy and fulfillment!

Thanks, I'm really excited to see where this project takes me. I've already learned so much from reading and talking to others, and I'm looking forward to sharing my own thoughts and ideas with the world.
I'm sure you'll do great! Remember to stay present and enjoy the journey, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need any help or guidance along the way.
